the mechanism ('dedup key is now derived from (order_id, event_id)'), and the consequence ('retries from the upstream provider are idempotent')

Verdict: keep ... Under the staccato contrast test, this is not contrast at all - it is a label list pointing at methodological choices the section has already described.
Each fragment is a technical label ('Held-out cases', 'Paired bootstrap', 'Sign-flip permutation') that the prior methods prose has already defined.
Concrete rewrite: No rewrite. Keep the three labels as-is.
Verdict: keep ... 'Useful writing is bold, but true.' is a short antithetical closer, so the staccato contrast test must be applied before flagging it.
Both poles of the contrast are already evidenced in the same paragraph: 'bold' was located on the demagogue side ... and 'true' was located on the academic side
Concrete rewrite: No rewrite. Keep 'Useful writing is bold, but true.' Expanding it into a longer explanatory sentence would ... forfeit the compression
Verdict: keep ... In context it is a cataphoric label: the next two sentences define each side.

'Loose' is discharged by 'I write the first draft of an essay fast, trying out all kinds of ideas.' 'Tight' is discharged by 'Then I spend days rewriting it very carefully.' Both poles of the contrast are evidenced in the same paragraph.
Demanding a tool name, metric, or external source here would invent specificity the paragraph does not need
Verdict: keep ... Under the staccato test, this is not parallel decorative closure - it is an escalation by concrete magnitude.
Each unit carries a distinct, concrete scope: a sentence, then 'whole branches of four or five paragraphs', then 'a whole essay'.
Concrete rewrite: No rewrite. Keep all three sentences. ... Collapsing the three into one summary sentence ... would strip the magnitudes
